stories.intro: US and Iran on the brink
stories.body: American forces struck Iranian port cities as the Revolutionary Guard declared the Strait of Hormuz closed, threatening the world's most critical oil chokepoint and raising fears of a wider war after Tehran moved to
Qatar and Oman are pushing urgent back-channel talks between Washington and Tehran, a diplomatic gamble to pull both sides back from open conflict before the confrontation spirals into a regional
The Trump administration has subpoenaed New York Times reporters over their Air Force One coverage, with the White House directing FBI Director Kash Patel to oversee an investigation into
A US judge has dismissed the criminal case against the Proud Boys following a Trump order, a striking demonstration of how executive power is being wielded to unwind accountability for
An inside account reveals how Secretary of State Marco Rubio has become the central architect of US policy toward Venezuela, effectively steering the country's fate from thousands of miles

A mass deportation campaign is sending hundreds of thousands of Afghans back across the border, straining an already fragile Taliban-run economy and raising urgent questions about what is truly driving
Thousands gathered in the Bosnian town to mark 31 years since Europe's worst massacre since World War II, a solemn reminder of genocide's long shadow as denialism resurfaces across
As international sanctions begin to lift, Syrians are cautiously eyeing an economic revival after years of collapse, weighing fragile optimism against the deep uncertainty of rebuilding
Moscow's galleries and stages remain vibrant with one unspoken rule: never mention Ukraine, a portrait of how a society sustains artistic life while carefully policing what cannot be
New research shows Einstein's relativistic effects directly govern how chemical bonds form in heavy elements, resolving a subtle puzzle about why certain metals behave the way
As autonomous weapons reshape modern warfare, engineers and soldiers are racing to develop countermeasures against a new generation of machines that can hunt human targets with unsettling
A close look at how Nvidia, CoreWeave and Nebius fund one another exposes a self-reinforcing loop propping up the GPU boom, raising questions about whether the AI infrastructure surge rests on
A Chinese voice actor found himself forced to demonstrate he wasn't an AI clone, a strange and revealing case of how synthetic voices are upending creative labor and identity in
A historical reckoning documents how the inventors of leaded gasoline knew it was toxic from the very first day, a case study in industrial denial whose public-health consequences echoed for
Researchers found that resistance training outperforms running for controlling blood sugar, a counterintuitive finding with real implications for how millions might better manage and prevent
An American rower has completed a historic solo journey from California to Hawaii, a feat of extraordinary endurance and navigation that stands as a testament to what one person can accomplish
